data: Four joining fragments of a marble plaque, of which the lower corners lost (and currently only two fragments located).
Rhomboid Ο, Θ, Φ; Α with broken bar. Presence of interpuncts and of a ligature of ΜΗ at l. 3
1st — 2nd century CE ('La datazione è difficile', Korhonen 2004: 214)
Observed by Ferrara and Kaibel in Museo Biscari, who saw the lower corners also; later observed by Korhonen in magazzino del cortile e magazzino superiore, Collezione Biscari (formerly sala VI.65).
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Castello Ursino, Piazza Federico II di Svevia, 95121 Catania (CT)
Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus

G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)
Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione
Kalle Korhonen, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione (Helsinki: Societas Scientiarum Fennica, 2004)
Storia di Catania sino alla fine del secolo XVIII

Text encoding: Victoria Fendel, Robert Crellin
ΙΟΥΛΙΑΓΕΡΜΑΝΑΗΘΕΟΦΙΛΗΣΕΖΗΣΕΝΕΤΗΙΔΜΗΝΑΣΙΑΗΜΕΡΑΣΙΕΙΟΥΛΙΟΣΓΕΡΜΑΝΟΣΓΛΥΚΥΤΑΤΗΘΥΓΑΤΡΙΕΠΟΙΗΣΕΝ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based on photographs and Kaibel's transcription ;
  • line.3: lapis, Ferrara, Kaibel: ΗΕ; Kaibel: η⟨ε⟩ (i.e. suppressed the E); Korhonen: ιε (or κε)

English translation

Marta Fogagnolo

Ioulia Germana, dear to the gods, lived for 14 years, 11 months and 15 days. Ioulios Germanos made this for his sweetest daughter.

Italian translation

Marta Fogagnolo

Ioulia Germana, cara agli dèi, visse 14 anni, 11 mesi e 15 giorni. Giulio Germano realizzò questo per la sua dolcissima figlia.

commentary

The structure of the epitaph is the traditional one for pagan epitaphs, with the name of the deceased (and an attribute), his/her age, the name of the dedicant, a noun describing the deceased (which often expresses the degree of kinship with the dedicant) in dative and the dedication verb (ποιέω in the aorist form), for which see Korhonen (2004: 85-87). The attribute θεοφιλής attributed to the deceased is uncommon in pagan Greek epigraphy, more frequent in Christian epigraphy in the superlative form as an attribute of bishops and emperors.

bibliography

  • Ferrara 1829 390 no. 3 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002005
  • Kaibel 1890 480 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001072
  • Korhonen 2004 100 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000028
  • Korhonen 2004 85-87 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000028

Text based on photographs and Kaibel's transcription lapis, Ferrara, Kaibel: ΗΕ; Kaibel: η⟨ε⟩ (i.e. suppressed the E); Korhonen: ιε (or κε)

Ioulia Germana, dear to the gods, lived for 14 years, 11 months and 15 days. Ioulios Germanos made this for his sweetest daughter.

Ioulia Germana, cara agli dèi, visse 14 anni, 11 mesi e 15 giorni. Giulio Germano realizzò questo per la sua dolcissima figlia.

The structure of the epitaph is the traditional one for pagan epitaphs, with the name of the deceased (and an attribute), his/her age, the name of the dedicant, a noun describing the deceased (which often expresses the degree of kinship with the dedicant) in dative and the dedication verb (ποιέω in the aorist form), for which see Korhonen (2004: 85-87). The attribute θεοφιλής attributed to the deceased is uncommon in pagan Greek epigraphy, more frequent in Christian epigraphy in the superlative form as an attribute of bishops and emperors.
